summary: Justin Brennan leads a mentorship session on setting up professional deal landing pages and pitch decks to attract investors. He demonstrates the technical backend of creating customized single-property landing pages within the CRM system, including embedding pitch decks and summary videos. The discussion covers capital raising strategy through investor relationship building rather than direct solicitation.
takeaways: Create a mock deal with landing page, pitch deck, and 5-10 minute summary video using Loom before pursuing real deals to practice the process
Use a single landing page (not full website) specific to each deal with unique URL saved on phone for quick sharing at networking events
Include embedded pitch deck, summary video, and property details on landing page so investors can self-educate before initial conversation
Never ask investors for money directly; instead invite them to opportunities and ask if they know anyone interested to shift psychology from sales to invitation
Send deal link immediately when interest is expressed ('money loves speed') rather than waiting until tomorrow
Use seller financing creatively (20% down, interest-only for 5 years at 5.5% fixed) as alternative to bank loans to improve deal economics
Keep renovation budget separate from loan financing when under $400k; only finance reno through debt when deal exceeds $400k threshold

skill_candidates.change: Implement landing page + pitch deck + video combo as standard capital raise tool before each deal
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin emphasizes this builds trust and professional credibility: 'only 2 out of 20 small GPs do this...sets you above the rest' and 'breeds confidence that you understand how to take property through this process'

skill_candidates.change: When broker numbers don't pencil with financing included, use deal analyzer conversation tool with broker rather than abandoning deal
skill_candidates.evidence: Johan's South Florida deals didn't work; Justin advises: 'Put in broker numbers working backwards to return—if not penciling, conversation is with broker. Say Yo Greg, I put in your numbers, am I missing something?'

skill_candidates.change: Expand geographic search beyond high-price markets like South Florida; pursue financed deals in Southeast markets where conventional financing works
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin tells Johan: 'South Florida market super tight...people buying appreciation. When growing wealth, can't be in wealth preservation mode. Open it up to Southeast where normal financing happening'

title: Tuesday Mentorship Q&A - How to Attract Investors Online_03-17-26 | Justin Brennan
